title: on_link_failure returns actions without deduplication
description:
File: python/src/lichen/loadng/error.py
Lines: 50-54

The `on_link_failure` method builds separate RerrAction for each destination:
```python
def on_link_failure(self, next_hop: IPv6Address | str) -> list[RerrAction]:
    nh = to_ipv6(next_hop)
    dests = set(self.cache.remove_via(nh)) | set(self.gradient.remove_via(nh))
    return [self._build_action(dest) for dest in sorted(dests, key=str)]
```

Problem: For each destination, a separate RERR message is created and each may notify the same precursor multiple times. If a precursor was forwarding traffic to destinations D1, D2, D3 all via the same failed next_hop, they receive 3 separate RERR notifications.

Impact:
1. Excessive network traffic during failures (O(n) messages instead of consolidated)
2. Precursor receives redundant information
3. LOADng spec allows batching multiple unreachable destinations in a single RERR (spec 10.6 mentions 'list of unreachable destinations')

Fix: Consolidate notifications by precursor:
```python
def on_link_failure(self, next_hop: IPv6Address | str) -> list[RerrAction]:
    nh = to_ipv6(next_hop)
    dests = set(self.cache.remove_via(nh)) | set(self.gradient.remove_via(nh))
    # Group by precursor to reduce message count
    precursor_to_dests: dict[IPv6Address, list[IPv6Address]] = {}
    for dest in dests:
        for precursor in self._precursors.pop(dest, set()):
            precursor_to_dests.setdefault(precursor, []).append(dest)
    # Build consolidated actions
    ...
```
close_reason: Implemented precursor-grouped deduplication in on_link_failure and extended _build_action for consolidated RerrActions with multiple invalidated destinations per notification. Tests and ruff pass. Commit 77fe8e4d2
